_about: Economic-layer config for the Bear River pilot. Routes one acre-foot of NEW water generated in the basin down the hydrologic cascade and attaches a marginal economic value ($/AF) at every place the water is used. Two value types: (1) CONSUMPTIVE nodes are rival — an acre-foot either grows a crop or flows on, never both — so their fractions sum to 1.0 (losses included) and there is no double-counting; (2) PASS-THROUGH nodes (hydropower, in-river recreation) are non-rival uses of water moving through the river, valued on throughflow without removing it; (3) LAKE-TERMINAL benefits are joint products of the same acre-foot that reaches the Great Salt Lake (the stock supports shrimp AND minerals AND dust suppression simultaneously), so they are summed per AF-to-lake, NOT partitioned. ALL dollar values are editable marginal/avoided-cost anchors from public sources (see economic-layer-plan.md) — illustrative, not Rainmaker company figures.

basin.name: Bear River
basin._inch_note: 1 inch of water over the whole basin = area_acres / 12 acre-feet.

partition_note: Default split leans toward runoff vs. a basin-average analog (~35% ET / 30% recharge / 30% runoff / 5% interception) because seeded water is high-elevation cool-season snow with a high runoff ratio. Total water reaching the lake (to_lake + baseflow return) = 0.27, consistent with the lumped model's 0.25 delivery factor. Every fraction is calibratable.

consumptive_partition.label: Crop transpiration (ET)
consumptive_partition.value_basis: USU GSL-basin ag water conservation cost $688-806/AF; alfalfa 3-5 AF/acre

consumptive_partition.label: Soil & fuel moisture
consumptive_partition.value_basis: Avoided suppression+loss; up to ~$505/acre fuel-treatment break-even (Hunter & Taylor)

consumptive_partition.label: Surface irrigation diversion
consumptive_partition.value_basis: Bear River Canal ~230K AF/yr serves 150K+ acres

consumptive_partition.label: Wetlands / Bird Refuge
consumptive_partition.value_basis: Bear River Migratory Bird Refuge, 8M birds; part is evaporative

lake_terminal.label: Dust / public-health avoided cost
lake_terminal.value_basis: Exposed-lakebed PM2.5/arsenic; Owens Lake analog; GSL lakebed = 23-34% Wasatch dust

lake_terminal.label: Brine shrimp, minerals, birds
lake_terminal.value_basis: Minerals $1.13B/5,368 jobs; brine shrimp $67M; GSL aggregate ~$2.5B/yr

lake_terminal.label: Lake-effect snow / ski
lake_terminal.value_basis: Lake adds 5-10% of UT snow, +5-7 ski weeks; ski $1.2B/20K jobs

lake_terminal_note: These are MARGINAL, illustrative per-AF avoided-cost/value anchors for water that actually reaches the lake. They are deliberately modest: seeding's contribution to lake LEVEL is 2-3 orders of magnitude below the structural shortfall (see the lumped model), so the defensible headline value sits upstream (agriculture, supply, wildfire), not in 'refilling the lake'.
